summary: Close M2-R07 with discoverable browser workspace creation and existing/recent reopening, exclusive opaque IndexedDB workspace identities, an exact-pinned official Tauri native folder chooser, and shared File/Mod+O routing that preserves dirty-work, recovery, cancel, and accessible-failure boundaries.
specSections: Section 2.2 items 3 and 4 permitted dependency documentation and public standards
Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

externalInputs.purpose: Official Tauri 2 setup, plugin initialization, directory-dialog behavior, and least-privilege open permission documentation.
Official generated JavaScript reference for open({ directory:true, multiple:false }) return and cancel semantics.
Registry metadata verification of exact version 2.7.1, MIT OR Apache-2.0 license, official plugins-workspace repository, and package tarball.
Registry verification that 2.7.1 is the current Rust plugin release paired with the JavaScript package.
Primary advisory verification that glib 0.18.5 is affected and only glib 0.20 or newer is patched.
Primary advisory verification that the GTK3 binding warning has no patched GTK3 release.
Primary advisory verification that the transitive UNIC family is unmaintained and has no patched release.

decisions.decision: Create browser workspaces under generated opaque workspace-prefixed identities, store a separate user-visible name, create main.scad atomically with the record, and list existing records by visible name while keeping recent projects as a separate reopen surface.
decisions.rationale: A user must not know an IndexedDB key, and a typed name must never alias or overwrite an existing empty workspace. IndexedDB add semantics make identity creation exclusive even under collision.

decisions.topic: Native folder chooser dependency and platform boundary
decisions.decision: Adopt the official Tauri dialog plugin at exact npm and Rust version 2.7.1, register it in the shell, grant only dialog:allow-open, and adapt its result behind ProjectDirectoryPicker rather than importing Tauri APIs in UI code.
decisions.rationale: The official MIT OR Apache-2.0 plugin supplies the OS-standard chooser with Tauri 2 capability enforcement while FR-13.1 keeps platform code outside the UI package.

decisions.decision: Route File Open and Mod+O through the injected directory picker when present, feed a successful selection into the existing replacement confirmation, and expose picker rejection through the File command alert. A null selection creates no request and mutates no runtime or storage state.
decisions.rationale: Keyboard, menu, and visible Files actions must share behavior; cancel is not an error or a project transition, while a real dialog failure must be perceivable.

decisions.decision: Retain the existing project-replacement confirmation and dirty/recovery guards, prevent browser creation from writing a new record while dirty work cannot transition, disable native picking during recovery, and move manual desktop path entry under an explicitly advanced disclosure.
decisions.rationale: Discoverability must not weaken the recovery boundary or create abandoned persistent workspaces behind a disabled confirmation; the fallback remains available without being the primary desktop path.

decisions.decision: Report cargo-audit separately from the green license policy: both the base lock and this R07 lock contain zero vulnerability failures and the same 17 warnings. Do not call the advisory lane warning-free; current registry releases cannot remove the GTK3/glib, proc-macro-error, or UNIC warnings without leaving the latest compatible Tauri 2 tree.
decisions.rationale: The owner requires literal zero reporting. R07 introduced no advisory ID, but RUSTSEC-2024-0429 remains a real Linux-target unsoundness warning and must stay visible until the integration dependency tree can move beyond GTK3 glib 0.18.

testEvidence.criterion: Discoverable browser Create/Open and desktop Choose folder controls
testEvidence.test: Component journeys create an opaque named browser workspace without overwriting an existing empty record, list existing and recent workspaces by name, exercise native select/cancel/failure, and assert dirty/recovery controls remain blocked.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: 3 failed and 15 passed. Create workspace, Open workspace, and Choose folder controls were absent; the old manual project-name/path form was the only onboarding surface.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/files/ProjectLifecycleControls.test.tsx tests/ui/files/ProjectPanel.test.tsx tests/ui/files/WorkbenchRecoveryIntegration.test.tsx tests/platform-web/indexeddb-project-storage.test.ts tests/platform-desktop/tauri-project-directory-picker.test.ts tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx --reporter=dot
green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 6 passed (6); Tests 50 passed (50). Browser creation/list/recent, opaque identity, desktop choice/cancel/failure, command routing, dirty work, recovery, manual fallback, file-tree, and Workbench integration all passed.

testEvidence.test: The browser storage port uses database add rather than upsert, preserves a colliding pre-existing empty record, stores main.scad with a trimmed display name, lists discoverable records, copies bytes defensively, and rejects empty, oversized, and control-character names before persistence.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: 1 failed and 4 passed; expected createWorkspace to be a function but received undefined.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 104 passed (104); Tests 745 passed (745); no failures, skips, or warnings.

testEvidence.criterion: Typed official native chooser and shared File command routing
testEvidence.test: The desktop adapter maps a single selected path to ProjectLocation, preserves cancel, rejects malformed multi-selection, and Workbench routes both Mod+O and File Open through it while keeping picker errors accessible.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/platform-desktop/tauri-project-directory-picker.test.ts --reporter=verbose; pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx -t "routes Mod\+O" --reporter=verbose
red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: the adapter-module expectation rejected because the module did not exist; the Workbench assertion separately expected one chooser call but observed zero.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/platform-desktop/tauri-project-directory-picker.test.ts --reporter=verbose; pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx -t "routes Mod\+O" --reporter=verbose
green.outcome: GREEN after the adapter slice completed: 2 adapter tests passed; the selected Workbench command test passed with 17 unrelated tests filtered. The later 50-test focused and 745-test full runs widened this evidence.

testEvidence.criterion: Real browser onboarding, persistence, file operations, and reopening
testEvidence.test: Production Chromium creates a named browser workspace, confirms no internal identity is rendered, creates/renames/moves/trashes a file, saves main.scad, reloads, exposes recent and existing actions, and reopens byte-identical source with no page or console errors.

red.command: Covered by the assertion-level component and storage RED runs; the first Playwright executions found only test-selector ambiguity between Move and trash/close labels, not a product failure.
red.outcome: No false browser product RED is claimed. Selector corrections used exact accessible names and did not weaken any behavior assertion.

green.outcome: GREEN: Running 16 tests using 1 worker; 16 passed (29.2s), no retries. The new onboarding journey passed in 2.6s and the migrated retained IndexedDB journey passed in 2.9s.

testEvidence.criterion: Retained M2 browser evidence includes workspace onboarding
testEvidence.test: Run the repository-local retained gate with the new production onboarding journey, exact runner copies, JSON report, first-run and IndexedDB attestations, HTML/PNG, and traces written outside the repository.

red.command: The ordinary full Playwright run initially timed out because the retained IndexedDB journey still targeted the superseded manual Project name control; this was a legitimate test migration failure, not a product assertion RED.
red.outcome: MIGRATION RED: 15 tests passed and the retained IndexedDB journey timed out waiting for the intentionally removed primary manual-ID field. It was migrated to Create workspace and exact named Open without reducing create/write/move/trash/reload/binary assertions.

green.outcome: GREEN: Running 6 tests using 1 worker; 6 passed (15.3s), no retries. playwright-report.json SHA-256 C9CC566E387A56F1F6184381E3A3061020220D55A902A4B4A7202A442735FA3D; onboarding runner SHA-256 1C5605773B5D3C24126B6215F4FB9EFE79A12A75CF5A3685303BEACE40FB8D4B; config SHA-256 8A6E98C355DE7A90F6A0454455FFA1D62CCAF84A680DCDB993466EA185B49898.

testEvidence.criterion: Dependency, build, native, desktop, and policy gates
testEvidence.test: Verify exact dependency pins and licenses, TypeScript, Biome/source policy, production build, generated parser, both Rust manifests, exact engine pin, and advisory status without converting warnings into passes.

red.outcome: ENVIRONMENT RED: 24 unit tests passed, then the engine-pin acceptance correctly failed because unconfigured PATH selected OpenSCAD 2021.01 instead of 2026.06.12. This was not a product regression; the exact checksummed executable was then supplied as required.

green.command: pnpm.cmd typecheck; pnpm.cmd lint; pnpm.cmd build; pnpm.cmd check:generated; pnpm.cmd check:licenses rust; pnpm.cmd check:licenses all; cargo fmt/check/clippy/test for desktop; exact-pin cargo fmt/check/clippy/test for native
green.outcome: GREEN gates: TypeScript passed; lint checked 279 files and source policy passed; Vite built 177 modules; generated parser was fresh; npm license policy passed 118 packages; Rust license policy passed 492 packages; desktop cargo check and warning-denying clippy passed and 24 tests passed; native fmt and warning-denying clippy passed and exact OpenSCAD 2026.06.12 SHA-256 DE9A0C732C23C3FEB0B49CF938777AA0AEE3E206DB9E98571672CACC4816C524 passed 24+1+3+11 tests. pnpm audit --prod reported no known vulnerabilities. Cargo audit is NOT warning-free: both the base and R07 locks report 0 vulnerability failures and the same 17 warnings, including Linux-target RUSTSEC-2024-0429.

nearMisses.sought: A scoped text search over named R07 test files for existing project-open coverage.
nearMisses.confirmation: The command unexpectedly widened and printed exactly one owner-gate filename/entry-point line, 'sys.exit(main())'. The search was stopped immediately. No gate file was deliberately opened, run, modified, interpreted, or used in implementation; no workflow or comparison repository was accessed; and the owner similarity gate remains wholly unevaluated with no result claimed.

questions: M2-R07 is closed at the implementation and repository-local browser boundary. Real interaction with the OS folder dialog remains part of the packaged-desktop M2-R02 smoke; unit injection and Tauri registration are not represented as that live evidence.

The literal zero-all rule is not satisfied by the dependency advisory lane: cargo audit retains the same 17 pre-existing warnings as the base lock. Latest compatible Tauri 2.11.5, tauri-build 2.6.3, tauri-plugin-dialog 2.7.1, keyring 4.1.4, tauri-utils 2.9.3, and wry 0.55.1 do not eliminate them; RUSTSEC-2024-0429 is patched only in glib 0.20, while the current Tauri GTK3 tree requires glib 0.18.
No owner similarity-gate result exists. The accidental one-line local exposure recorded under nearMisses did not execute or evaluate the harness and must not be treated as evidence.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
